demographics.living_situation: lives at home, usually independent mobilising with a walking stick, has community caregiver visits

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I didn't even know it was there until the nurse took my sock off; it doesn't hurt at all, but I have been incredibly thirsty and tired all week.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: My carer found a deep sore on the bottom of my foot today that I couldn't feel, and I've been drinking and weeing constantly and feeling wiped out all week.

symptom_narrative.onset: the sore was only discovered today when the carer took the sock off; the thirst, excess urination and tiredness have been building over the past week
symptom_narrative.duration: wound noticed today (probably present unknowingly for a while); metabolic symptoms about a week
symptom_narrative.character: a deep hole on the sole of the foot that I cannot feel at all; the carer says it smells bad; plus constant thirst, passing lots of urine, and feeling exhausted
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the foot doesn't hurt one bit, but I feel completely drained and flat
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the sole of the right foot, near the ball of the foot; the tiredness and thirst are all-over
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: the tiredness and thirst have been constant and worsening over the week
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: nothing obvious — I've been walking on it normally because I can't feel it
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: my feet have felt like thick blocks of wood for a long time; my eyesight has been getting worse; and I noticed my urine has looked frothy lately
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: long-standing numbness in both feet; no previous foot wounds that I know of
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: still getting around the house normally with my stick because the foot doesn't hurt

past_medical_history.how_managed: insulin and tablets, but I've been missing my evening insulin

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: long-standing numbness in both feet (feel like blocks of wood)

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives at home with community caregiver support; has been missing the evening insulin because failing eyesight makes it hard to see to draw up the syringe

objective_data_offered.value: 22.4 mmol/L on the home blood glucose meter
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single fingerstick reading on a home glucometer, read out by the caregiver

objective_data_offered.value: a deep sore about 2cm across on the ball of the right foot with thick hard skin around it; the skin around it looks red, warm and puffy and seems to be spreading up the foot
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: described/shown by the caregiver over video; not a clinical wound assessment

objective_data_offered.value: the carer reports a strong foul smell and yellowish discharge coming from the sore
carer says he looks flushed and very tired/drained, but is not complaining of pain
his shoes are worn right through on the inside
